data.title: Strategy purchased $255 million worth of Bitcoin over the past week at an average price of $77,906.
data.description: Strategy has spent approximately $255 million (about $77,906 per coin) to purchase 3,273 Bitcoins. As of April 26, 2026, Strategy held 818,334 Bitcoins, with a total value of approximately $61.81 billion (about $75,537 per coin).

data.title: Spark Protocol Reports Financial Results for Q1 2026
data.description:
DeFi lending protocol Spark has released its financial report for the first quarter of 2026. According to Foresight News, the report reveals that the total revenue for Q1 was $31.5 million, marking a 31% decrease compared to the previous quarter. The net protocol income stood at $6.91 million, down by 30%, while the net protocol surplus was $3.46 million, a 47% decline. By the end of the quarter, the protocol's treasury reached $46.1 million, reflecting a 5.7% increase.

Additionally, Spark initiated its SPK token buyback program for the first time this quarter, utilizing 986,000 USDS for open market repurchases. During this period, reward distribution surpassed the net income from Spark's liquidity layer (SLL), becoming the primary source of net income for the protocol, with total quarterly revenue of $3.31 million and a distribution supply of $4.5 billion.

Regarding the SLL, the average deployed capital was $1.93 billion, with an average annualized yield of 5.8%. However, due to weakened DeFi lending demand, the captured interest spread narrowed from 0.83% in January to 0.41% in March. SparkLend's reserve factor income for the quarter was $156,000, with USDT deposit balances reaching $285 million by the end of March.

data.title: Iran Considers Compensation Plan for Damages by Foreign Governments
data.description: Iran's Islamic Parliament is considering a plan that would require foreign governments to compensate for damages caused to Iran in recent years. According to Odaily, Ibrahim Azizi, Chairman of the National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, stated that the plan targets countries that have harmed Iran, frozen its resources, or taken actions against it. The proposal is currently in draft form and will be submitted to the parliamentary plenary session for review to become law.

data.title: Crypto Developer Reflects on Ongoing Legal Challenges Under Trump Administration
data.description: Keonne Rodriguez, in a letter from FPC Morgantown, marked two years since his arrest on April 24, 2024. According to NS3.AI, Rodriguez expressed that policy changes under the Trump administration have not halted legal proceedings against crypto developers, including himself, Bill, and Roman Storm.

data.title: Iranian official: Countries that have caused harm to Iran need to provide compensation.
data.description: Ibrahim Aziz, chairman of the National Security and Foreign Policy Committee of the Iranian Islamic Parliament, said the proposed plan would require governments that have harmed Iran, frozen Iranian resources, or taken actions against Iran in recent years to pay compensation. According to him, the plan is currently a draft and will be submitted to the plenary session of parliament for review and to become law. (Iranian International Television)

data.title: Pakistan's Central Bank Warns of Potential Inflation Surge
data.description: Pakistan's central bank has indicated that current supply shocks could drive inflation into double digits in the coming months. According to Jin10, the bank expects these pressures to ease thereafter.

data.title: Pakistan's Central Bank Expects Inflation to Remain Above Target
data.description: Pakistan's central bank has projected that inflation will remain above the upper limit of its target range of 5% to 7% for most of the fiscal year 2027. According to Jin10, this outlook reflects ongoing economic challenges that the country faces.

data.title: Pakistan's Central Bank Forecasts GDP Growth Near Lower Range for 2026 Fiscal Year
data.description: Pakistan's central bank has projected that the country's real GDP growth rate for the 2026 fiscal year will likely approach the lower end of its previously forecasted range. According to Jin10, the trend of slowing economic activity is expected to continue into the 2027 fiscal year.

data.title: Crypto Exchanges Diversify Expansion Models into Traditional Finance
data.description: Crypto exchanges are diversifying their expansion into traditional finance, adopting five distinct architectural models rather than a single approach. According to PANews, these models are expected to coexist rather than converge, reflecting a varied strategy in integrating with the traditional financial sector.

data.title: The founder of Curve proposed using a market-based model to recover bad DeFi debt.
data.description: Curve founder Michael Egorov proposed recovering bad debts in lending protocols by converting damaged positions into tradable investment products. The proposal is being piloted on Curve's own CRV-long LlamaLend market, which generated approximately $700,000 in bad debts in October 2025. Michael Egorov has established a Curve Stableswap pool with approximately 71% solvency, allowing trading of damaged vault tokens. Traders can buy at a discount, liquidity providers can earn fees, and the DAO can accumulate damaged tokens through management fees. Currently, community feedback on this proposal is mixed, with some users questioning whether the lack of immediate returns on damaged positions will attract buyers.

data.title: France has charged 88 suspects in a cryptocurrency wrench attack, including more than 10 minors.
data.description: The French National Prosecutor's Office for Organized Crime (PNACO) announced on Friday that France has launched judicial investigations into 12 cases of cryptocurrency kidnapping orchestrated by organized crime groups, and has charged 88 suspects, including more than 10 minors. According to statistics, since 2023, France has recorded 135 cryptocurrency-related attacks, with 18 in 2024, 67 in 2025, and 47 so far in 2026. Those charged face charges including kidnapping, unlawful detention, extortion, and money laundering. Recently, police arrested six suspects in two separate kidnapping operations; all are currently in preventative custody. Jonathan Riss, a blockchain intelligence analyst at CertiK, stated that the masterminds of such criminal groups are typically located outside the EU.

data.title: Vessel tracking data: Approximately seven vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours.
data.description: Ship tracking data shows that approximately seven vessels, mainly bulk carriers and cargo ships, passed through the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours. (Jinshi)

data.title: Chinese Man Detained for Allegedly Torturing Stray Cats for Crypto Payments
data.description:
Chinese authorities have detained a man from Henan province accused of torturing approximately 1,500 stray cats to death in exchange for cryptocurrency payments. According to NS3.AI, the individual, identified as Ma, has been placed under administrative detention for a period of 10 days. A rescue organization reportedly spent several months connecting Ma to a private Telegram group that allegedly commissioned custom videos of cat torture.

Legal experts have noted that the case may present challenges for prosecutors due to the ambiguous legal protections for stray cats in China. A lawyer speaking to China Newsweek highlighted the potential complexities involved in pursuing charges against Ma, given the current legal framework surrounding animal welfare.

data.title: Pakistan's Virtual Asset Authority Clarifies Regulations on Stablecoin Cross-Border Payments
data.description: Pakistan's Virtual Asset Regulatory Authority (PVARA) has issued a statement regarding recent announcements by financial institutions about pilot programs involving stablecoin cross-border payments and remittances. According to Foresight News, PVARA emphasized that under the 2026 Virtual Asset Act, providing virtual asset services within Pakistan, including issuance, transfer, custody, exchange, and related blockchain solutions, falls under its regulatory jurisdiction. Any agreements or pilot programs must receive prior authorization from PVARA. Plans announced without prior communication may face regulatory, reputational, and FATF compliance risks, and related activities may not be legally conducted. The authority advises stakeholders to engage with them early through processes such as regulatory sandboxes or No Objection Certificates (NOC).

data.title: Seven Ships Transit Hormuz Strait in 24 Hours
data.description: Approximately seven vessels, primarily bulk carriers and cargo ships, have passed through the Hormuz Strait in the last 24 hours, according to Odaily.

data.title: Putin to Meet Iranian Foreign Minister in St. Petersburg Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is scheduled to meet with Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi in St. Petersburg, according to Odaily. Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov announced the meeting on the 27th, highlighting its significance given the current situation in the Middle East.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Analysts Note Rebound in Investor Risk Appetite
data.description: Investor risk appetite has returned to levels seen before the Iran conflict, according to Goldman Sachs analysts. ChainCatcher reports that the stock market's volatility has significantly decreased as investors bet against the worst-case scenario in the U.S.-Iran tensions. Retail investors in the U.S. are re-entering the stock market, and automated trading is contributing to the market's rise. In the four weeks leading up to last Wednesday, investors withdrew nearly $125 billion from money market funds, marking one of the largest outflows on record.

data.title: Strive CEO Responds to Grayscale Report on Bitcoin's Impact on Dividends
data.description: Strive CEO ColeMacro has responded to a Grayscale report, highlighting its assessment of Strategy's ability to pay long-term STRC dividends based on Bitcoin price increases. According to ChainCatcher, ColeMacro emphasized the significant demand for digital credit, noting its double-digit returns, low volatility, and appeal to investors who prefer credit risk investments. He also criticized the report for not addressing volatility and lacking in credit analysis.

data.title: Strive CEO responds to Grayscale report: STRC dividends depend on Bitcoin price increases.
data.description: Strive CEO ColeMacro responded to the Grayscale report, stating that it indicated Strategy's long-term ability to pay STRC dividends depends on rising Bitcoin prices. Macro stated that there is huge demand for digital credit because it offers double-digit returns, low volatility, and is easily understood and favored by credit risk investors. He also pointed out that the report failed to discuss volatility and had gaps in its credit analysis. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: ING Analysts Warn of Slowing Potential Growth in Eurozone
data.description: ING analysts Ruben Dewitt and Peter Vanden Houte have indicated that potential growth in the Eurozone is likely to slow due to suppressed productivity. According to Jin10, the analysts stated in a report that unless there is a sudden acceleration in productivity, the potential GDP growth rate could fall below 1%. They highlighted that while productivity has been a significant driver of growth in the United States, it remains weak in Europe. Italy and Germany even experienced negative productivity growth during 2024-25. Expanding the labor force could be a key driver, as evidenced by increased immigration in Spain. However, they noted that due to political resistance, this is unlikely to serve as a model for other parts of Europe. Revitalizing productivity will require ambitious structural policies, but progress is expected to be very slow.
